title: Polypharmacy and anticholinergic burden in older adults
key_recommendations: Anticholinergic Cognitive Burden (ACB) score >= 3 is associated with cognitive impairment, falls, and mortality in older adults. Each point increase confers measurable additional risk.
Common offenders: diphenhydramine, oxybutynin, amitriptyline, hydroxyzine, paroxetine, scopolamine.
Beers Criteria 2023 specifically discourages: long-term benzodiazepines, first-generation antihistamines, tertiary tricyclic antidepressants in patients >65.
Before adding new symptomatic therapy (e.g., cholinesterase inhibitor for suspected dementia), deprescribe sedating and anticholinergic medications and reassess after 6-8 weeks.
Orthostatic hypotension in elderly is iatrogenic until proven otherwise.
